content.claim: Between-school variance in Hungary is the third highest among all OECD countries, while within-school variance is below the OECD average, indicating that early tracking concentrates educational inequality between schools rather than within them.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: High between-school variance reflects the outcome of tracking but does not establish whether earlier tracking age directly causes greater variance than later selection.
Within-school variance being low may reflect either pedagogical benefits of homogeneous grouping or reduced opportunity for differentiation.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
